title: Where Runoff Becomes Record

features.name: confluence-pool
features.desc-on-touch: where the two paddocks' runoff meets, their waters still slightly separate in colour and temperature. you see the mark where memory from upstream-left teaches memory from upstream-right.

features.name: flow-testimony-post
features.desc-on-touch: a scarred wooden post marked with flow lines, each line a season, each season a different observer's handwriting. the archive's way of saying: this is what we measured, together.

features.name: collection-funnel
features.desc-on-touch: a fine mesh funnel catches where the waters merge fully, holding samples of the convergence. the archive's own small library of becoming-one.

features.name: upstream-voice-echo
features.desc-on-touch: the sound of the culvert reaches here doubled—one tributary whispers a different place-memory than the other. the archive hears them both at once.

features.name: downstream-bend-marker
features.desc-on-touch: where the confluence turns, the water's surface catches light and memory together. a turning-point, briefly visible before the water continues underground.

inscription: two fields remember separately until they meet in water, then they remember together

file.name: Dual_Paddock_Runoff_Stereo_Recording_NSW_1987.mp3
file.note: the same storm on two different fields, separated by a ridge, captured as stereo: left the paddock above, right the paddock beside—convergence is silence between them

exits.label: back into the culvert's deeper passage
downstream, where the joined memory learns what stone remembers
upslope to where one memory originated, before the meeting
deeper downstream, toward where all region's waters gather
